# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

# Use an older version of Ubuntu that still supported Python 2.7 by default
FROM ubuntu:16.04

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED 1

# Update package list and install necessary build tools and Python development headers
# These are often required for compiling Python packages with C extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        build-essential \
        python-dev \
        python-pip \
        libssl-dev \
        libffi-dev \
        libpq-dev \
        git && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Upgrade pip to a version that can install from modern PyPI (if needed, adjust as per your legacy needs)
RUN pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the application requirements file into the container
# This allows caching of the pip install step if requirements.txt doesn't change
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install application dependencies
# Use 'pip install -r' to install all packages listed in requirements.txt
# Using --no-cache-dir can save space but might slow down future rebuilds if not leveraging Docker layer caching well
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code into the container
COPY . .

# Expose the port your application listens on (change if your app uses a different port)
EXPOSE 8000

# Command to run the application
# Replace 'your_app.py' with the actual entry point of your Python 2.7 application
# And 'python' with 'python2' if your base image has both python2 and python3 executables
CMD ["python", "your_app.py"]
